Incident Report: Kanzaki OriTech K.K. (Issuer) – Cloud Log Exposure on 2026-05-31; prepared under APPI and FIEA compliance review
A misconfigured object store at vendor Blue Crane Cloud G.K. briefly exposed application logs containing product manuals (SakuraShield Gate, KumoDrive Sync, Hinode Router), DEI messaging, and policy text, but no customer transaction data
Review of exposed text indicates mostly public content (Sustainability Update 2025, Respectful Workplace Charter, Inclusive Hiring Guidelines) and generic training slide decks with sports/places metaphors like “Aoba Meteors defense zone” and “Kawabe Comets playbook”
APPI scope check: no “Individual Number” (My Number) or “individual identification codes” were present; only template labels such as “My Number: XXXX-XXXX-XXXX (enter only if required by law)” appeared in blank onboarding forms
During internal triage, employee Rinka Yabuta reported to HR that she is receiving outpatient treatment for asthma to adjust her shift schedule; this special-category health information was confined to an HR ticket and was not part of the exposed logs
Board and acquisition context: board-approved share acquisition of Midorigaoka NanoParts K.K. was announced on 2026-03-10 and filed publicly; one log referenced the public press release only, and no draft valuations or undisclosed terms were present
Securities workflow artifacts included a “Securities Processing Checklist” with placeholders (TOB Ref: TOB-0000-JP; ISIN: JPH0TEST0001; Ticker: HNX0), clearly marked as samples for training and not tied to live transactions
Issuer/exchange context: Kanzaki OriTech K.K. (issuer code HN-4821X) is listed on the Hinomaru Growth Exchange; our review concluded no need for timely disclosure as no undisclosed enumerated material facts under FIEA were implicated
Employee workflow note: role mailbox hr-ops@kanzaki-oritech.example.jp and the public helpdesk 0120-000-000 handled two questions about DEI language reuse; no personal email addresses or direct phone numbers were stored in the affected logs
Policy inventory in the logs included APPI Response SOP v2.1, My Number Handling Standard v3.2, and Vendor Security Baseline v1.9; these documents contain only general guidance and do not embed identity data beyond example fields
Technical detail: exposed bucket contained rotated debug logs for SakuraShield Gate staging nodes (Cluster “Dojo-A”) and a knowledge base page titled “Stadium Topologies,” both lacking user payloads
FIEA/MNPI check: no undisclosed enumerated material facts or tender-offer facts were stored or accessed; references to the Midorigaoka NanoParts K.K. acquisition were limited to already-announced facts
Remediation: Blue Crane Cloud G.K. removed public ACLs, rotated API keys, and confirmed via audit hash that production PII stores and the My Number enclave were not logged; Kanzaki OriTech K.K. enforced policy re-training for developers
Regulatory posture: based on APPI thresholds and Hinomaru Growth Exchange guidelines, this event does not trigger breach notification; we will file an advisory note with the Shirasagi Prefecture Data Liaison Office for transparency only
